issues.description: `create_solution_map` is a verbatim duplicate of the existing `build_solved_values` helper (lines 55-74). Both functions take `&[AutoParam]` and `&[f64]`, assert equal lengths with the same failure message, and build a `HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` of `Value::Scalar` entries using `dimension_of(&spec.param_type)`. The only differences are the function name, parameter identifiers (`specifications`/`solution_values` vs. `params`/`x`), and closure-local names (`spec`/`computed_val` vs. `param`/`val`). There is no behavioral difference, no new capability, and no new test coverage. This directly violates the DRY principle that `build_solved_values` was extracted to enforce (see prior task `Solver: extract build_solved_values helper to deduplicate zip-insert loop` in tasks.json).
issues.suggested_fix: Delete the new `create_solution_map` function entirely. If some caller needs this behavior, call the existing `build_solved_values(&problem.auto_params, &x)` — all three current call sites (lines 537, 662, 697) already use it.

issues.description: `create_solution_map` has zero callers anywhere in the workspace (verified via grep across `crates/`, tests, and the whole repo — no hits). It is a private `fn` with no `#[allow(dead_code)]`, so Rust will emit `warning: function `create_solution_map` is never used`. Reify's lint gate is `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` (orchestrator.yaml:15, hooks/project-checks:30), which promotes warnings to hard errors. This diff will therefore fail clippy/CI the moment it lands and block the merge queue.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the function. If the intent was to rename `build_solved_values` to `create_solution_map`, do the rename in place (and update the 3 call sites and 4 unit tests at lines 537, 662, 697, 2335-2464) rather than adding a second copy. But note the existing name is consistent with the sibling helper `build_trial_values`, so the rename is not recommended.

issues.description: Even if the function were retained, it has no unit tests. The sibling `build_solved_values` is exercised by four unit tests (lines 2335, 2405, 2413, 2451) covering happy path, empty input, dimensionless types, and length-mismatch panic. A new helper with identical semantics and no tests is an inconsistency.
issues.suggested_fix: Delete the duplicate (preferred). If kept, add equivalent tests — but duplicating the tests would compound the duplication problem, which reinforces the primary finding: remove the function.

issues.description: The name `create_solution_map` diverges from the established naming convention in this file. The existing helpers use the `build_*_values` prefix (`build_solved_values`, `build_trial_values`), and the return type is a `HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` — the project uses the term `ValueMap` for related types but this helper returns a `HashMap`, not a `PersistentMap`-based `ValueMap`. `create_solution_map` is both a naming-convention regression and slightly misleading about the return type.
issues.suggested_fix: Moot if the function is deleted as recommended. Otherwise, keep the name `build_solved_values`.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: This diff adds a new private helper `create_solution_map` that is a 100% functional duplicate of the existing `build_solved_values` helper in the same file — identical signature, identical logic, identical assertion message, only local identifiers renamed. The new function has zero call sites anywhere in the workspace, which will trigger Rust's `dead_code` warning; because reify's lint gate runs `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` (orchestrator.yaml:15, hooks/project-checks:30), this diff will fail CI and cannot merge as-is. There is also no test coverage for the new helper, whereas `build_solved_values` has four dedicated unit tests. The correct fix is to delete `create_solution_map` entirely and let the three existing call sites continue using `build_solved_values`. This diff appears to undo the very deduplication that the prior task (`Solver: extract build_solved_values helper to deduplicate zip-insert loop`) was created to achieve.
